title: Neן¿½raן¿½s Wreath
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Neן¿½ra crowns me with a purple wreath
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ That she with her own dainty hands did twine;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Gold-hearted blossoms and blue buds in sheath,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mingled with veined green leaves of the wild vine.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Then, bending down her bright head, ah, too nigh!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ She asks me for a song: the daylight dies:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The song is still unwritten: still I lie
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Watching the purple twilight of her eyes.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I am her laureate; therefore heart of grace
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I take to kiss her. Where was song like this?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Love is best sung of in a loveless place,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For who would care to sing where he might kiss?
meaning: About: Victor James William Patrick Daley (5 September 1858 ן¿½ 29 December 1905) was an Australian poet and journalist.
